query: How do I install a curtain tie-back hook?
goal: Install a secure curtain tie-back hook at an appropriate wall position and verify that it holds the curtain.

steps.text: Choose, measure, and mark the hook position.
completion_cues: A suitable wall position is measured and clearly marked.
incompletion_cues: The position is not chosen, measured, or marked.

steps.text: Prepare the mounting holes and anchors required by the wall.
completion_cues: Mounting holes and any required anchors are ready.
incompletion_cues: The wall is not prepared or the anchors are missing or loose.

steps.text: Attach and tighten the tie-back hook.
completion_cues: The hook is aligned and firmly fastened to the wall.
incompletion_cues: The hook is detached, misaligned, or not tight.

steps.text: Place the curtain in the tie-back and test the installation.
completion_cues: The hook holds the curtain securely and remains aligned under a gentle test.
incompletion_cues: The curtain is not tested or the hook shifts or fails to hold.

query: How do I replace a toilet paper holder spring rod?
task: Replacing a toilet paper holder spring rod
goal: Replace the toilet paper holder spring rod and verify that the new rod operates and holds a roll correctly.

steps.text: Inspect the holder and prepare the compatible replacement rod and tools.
completion_cues: The holder is accessible and the replacement rod and needed tools are ready.
incompletion_cues: The holder has not been inspected or the replacement and tools are not ready.

steps.text: Compress and remove the old spring rod and roll.
completion_cues: The old rod is fully removed without damaging the holder.
incompletion_cues: The old rod remains engaged or the holder is being forced incorrectly.

steps.text: Load the roll and seat the new spring rod in both holder slots.
completion_cues: Both ends of the new rod are seated and the roll is centered.
incompletion_cues: An end is not seated or the roll and rod are misaligned.

steps.text: Test the spring action and roll rotation and make final adjustments.
completion_cues: The rod compresses and rebounds, stays installed, and lets the roll turn.
incompletion_cues: The rod slips, binds, or has not been tested.

query: How do I create a simple comic strip with 3 panels?
task: Creating a simple comic strip (3 panels)
goal: Create a clear three-panel comic strip with a simple beginning, development, and ending.

steps.text: Choose the short story and plan what happens in each of the three panels.
completion_cues: A coherent three-part story and panel sequence are decided.
incompletion_cues: The story or the role of one or more panels is undecided.

steps.text: Lay out three panels and sketch the main characters and actions.
completion_cues: All three panel frames contain recognizable sketched action.
incompletion_cues: Panel frames or essential sketches are missing.

steps.text: Add dialogue, captions, and final line work or color.
completion_cues: The panels contain readable text and sufficiently finished artwork.
incompletion_cues: Dialogue, captions, or final visual details remain incomplete.

steps.text: Review panel order and readability and finish the comic.
completion_cues: The three panels read in order and the completed story is clear.
incompletion_cues: The sequence is confusing, text is unreadable, or unfinished marks remain.

query: How do I replace a faucet washer?
goal: Replace the worn faucet washer, reassemble the faucet, and verify that it no longer leaks.

steps.text: Shut off the water, protect the drain, and expose the faucet handle assembly.
completion_cues: Water is isolated and the handle or cover is ready for disassembly.
incompletion_cues: Water remains on, the drain is unprotected, or the assembly is inaccessible.

steps.text: Disassemble the valve and remove the old washer.
completion_cues: The valve stem is accessible and the worn washer is removed.
incompletion_cues: The old washer remains installed or parts are removed unsafely.

steps.text: Fit the correct new washer and reassemble the faucet.
completion_cues: The new washer is seated and all faucet parts are correctly reassembled.
incompletion_cues: The washer is mis-seated or the assembly is incomplete or loose.

steps.text: Restore the water and test the faucet for operation and leaks.
completion_cues: The faucet operates normally and no leak is visible during the test.
incompletion_cues: Water has not been restored and tested or a leak remains.

query: How do I create a coin bank from a milk carton?
task: Creating a coin bank from a milk carton
goal: Turn a clean milk carton into a usable, safely finished coin bank.

steps.text: Clean and dry the carton and plan the coin slot and access opening.
completion_cues: The carton is clean and dry and the openings are marked.
incompletion_cues: The carton is not prepared or the openings are not planned.

steps.text: Cut the coin slot and any access opening safely.
completion_cues: A usable coin slot and planned access opening are cut with safe edges.
incompletion_cues: The openings are missing, incorrectly sized, or unsafe.

steps.text: Seal, shape, and decorate the carton body.
completion_cues: The carton holds its form and its exterior is securely covered or decorated.
incompletion_cues: The body is open, weak, or only partly decorated.

steps.text: Finish the openings and test the bank with coins.
completion_cues: Coins enter through the slot, remain inside, and can be retrieved as intended.
incompletion_cues: The bank is not tested or coins cannot be stored or retrieved correctly.

query: How do I make baked wonton chips?
goal: Bake crisp, evenly seasoned wonton chips without burning them.

steps.text: Preheat the oven and cut and season the wonton wrappers.
completion_cues: The oven is heating and the wrappers are cut and evenly coated or seasoned.
incompletion_cues: The oven is not prepared or wrappers remain uncut or unseasoned.

steps.text: Arrange the wrapper pieces in a single layer on a baking tray.
completion_cues: The pieces are spaced in one even layer on a prepared tray.
incompletion_cues: Pieces overlap, crowd the tray, or are not arranged for baking.

steps.text: Bake and monitor the chips until crisp and lightly browned.
completion_cues: The chips are crisp and evenly lightly browned without burning.
incompletion_cues: The chips are underbaked, uneven, unmonitored, or burning.

steps.text: Cool, taste, and serve or store the chips.
completion_cues: The chips have cooled enough to crisp and are ready to serve or store.
incompletion_cues: The chips are still hot and soft or have not been checked and finished.

query: How do I organize a drawer?
goal: Organize a drawer so its contents are grouped, visible, and easy to retrieve.

steps.text: Empty the drawer and sort its contents into keep, relocate, and discard groups.
completion_cues: The drawer is empty and every item has an initial category.
incompletion_cues: Items remain unsorted in the drawer or unwanted items are not separated.

steps.text: Clean the drawer and group kept items by use or type.
completion_cues: The drawer interior is clean and kept items form clear functional groups.
incompletion_cues: The drawer is dirty or kept items remain mixed without categories.

steps.text: Choose dividers or zones and return the grouped items.
completion_cues: Each group has a suitable, accessible place in the drawer.
incompletion_cues: The layout is undecided or items are returned without stable grouping.

steps.text: Check access and drawer movement and make final adjustments.
completion_cues: Important items are visible and accessible and the drawer opens and closes freely.
incompletion_cues: Items are hidden, overcrowded, unstable, or obstruct drawer movement.

query: How do I make a spinach and feta quesadilla?
task: Making a spinach and feta quesadilla
goal: Cook a crisp spinach and feta quesadilla with an evenly warmed filling.

steps.text: Gather and prepare the tortillas, spinach, feta, and cooking equipment.
completion_cues: All ingredients are measured or prepared and the pan and utensils are ready.
incompletion_cues: Essential ingredients or equipment are missing or unprepared.

steps.text: Cook or wilt the spinach and season the filling.
completion_cues: The spinach is wilted, excess moisture is controlled, and the filling is seasoned.
incompletion_cues: The spinach is raw, watery, overcooked, or not seasoned.

steps.text: Assemble the quesadilla with an even spinach and feta filling.
completion_cues: The filling is distributed inside a closed or folded tortilla without overfilling.
incompletion_cues: The filling is missing, uneven, outside the tortilla, or excessive.

steps.text: Cook both sides, portion, and serve the quesadilla.
completion_cues: Both sides are crisp and browned, the filling is hot, and the quesadilla is ready to serve.
incompletion_cues: A side is uncooked or burned, the filling is cold, or serving is incomplete.

query: How do I make marshmallow treats?
goal: Make evenly mixed marshmallow cereal treats that set firmly and can be cut cleanly.

steps.text: Prepare the pan and ingredients and melt the butter and marshmallows smoothly.
completion_cues: The pan is prepared and the binder is fully melted and smooth.
incompletion_cues: The pan is unprepared or marshmallows remain unmelted, scorched, or lumpy.

steps.text: Combine the cereal with the melted marshmallow mixture.
completion_cues: The cereal is evenly coated with the marshmallow binder.
incompletion_cues: Dry cereal or unmixed binder remains or the mixture is being crushed excessively.

steps.text: Transfer and press the mixture evenly into the prepared pan.
completion_cues: The mixture forms a compact, level layer reaching the pan edges.
incompletion_cues: The mixture is not transferred or is loose, uneven, or incomplete in the pan.

steps.text: Let the treats set, then cut and serve them.
completion_cues: The slab has set and is cut into intact portions ready to serve.
incompletion_cues: The mixture is still unset or portions cannot be cut cleanly.

query: How do I make chili-lime roasted peanuts?
goal: Make evenly roasted chili-lime peanuts with balanced seasoning and no scorching.

steps.text: Preheat the oven and combine the peanuts with chili, lime, and other seasonings.
completion_cues: The oven is heating and all peanuts are evenly coated with seasoning.
incompletion_cues: The oven is not prepared or peanuts and seasonings remain unevenly mixed.

steps.text: Spread the seasoned peanuts evenly on a baking tray.
completion_cues: The peanuts form an even, uncrowded layer ready to roast.
incompletion_cues: The peanuts remain piled, clumped, or off the prepared tray.

steps.text: Roast the peanuts, stirring and monitoring for even browning.
completion_cues: The peanuts are fragrant and evenly roasted without scorching.
incompletion_cues: Roasting is incomplete, uneven, unmonitored, or burning.

steps.text: Cool the peanuts, adjust the seasoning, and serve or store them.
completion_cues: The peanuts are cooled, crisp, balanced in flavor, and ready to serve or store.
incompletion_cues: The peanuts are still hot or soft or have not been checked and finished.

query: How do I create a paper pop-up card element?
task: Creating a paper pop-up card element
goal: Create a paper pop-up element that folds flat and opens reliably inside a card.

steps.text: Choose the pop-up design and mark the fold and cut lines.
completion_cues: The element shape, attachment points, folds, and cuts are clearly planned.
incompletion_cues: The design or required fold and cut lines are missing or unclear.

steps.text: Cut and crease the pop-up element and its support tabs.
completion_cues: The element is cut cleanly and all intended folds and tabs are creased.
incompletion_cues: Cuts or creases are incomplete, misplaced, or damaged.

steps.text: Align and attach the tabs inside the card without gluing moving parts.
completion_cues: The tabs are firmly attached at the intended positions and the element can move.
incompletion_cues: The element is misaligned, loose, or immobilized by adhesive.

steps.text: Open and close the card, adjust the mechanism, and finish the design.
completion_cues: The element repeatedly pops up and folds flat without catching, and the card is finished.
incompletion_cues: The mechanism catches, tears, remains raised when closed, or is untested.

query: How do I lubricate a sticky drawer track?
goal: Lubricate a sticky drawer track so the drawer slides smoothly without excess residue.

steps.text: Empty and remove the drawer and inspect and clean the tracks.
completion_cues: The tracks are accessible, debris is removed, and damage has been checked.
incompletion_cues: The drawer or contents obstruct access or the tracks remain dirty or uninspected.

steps.text: Select a suitable lubricant and apply a thin, even amount to the contact surfaces.
completion_cues: The correct track surfaces have a light, controlled coating of suitable lubricant.
incompletion_cues: Lubricant is missing, unsuitable, applied to the wrong area, or excessive.

steps.text: Reinstall and cycle the drawer to distribute lubricant and assess movement.
completion_cues: The drawer is correctly reinstalled and moves repeatedly with less resistance.
incompletion_cues: The drawer is misaligned, not cycled, or still sticks substantially.

steps.text: Wipe away excess lubricant, reload the drawer, and perform a final test.
completion_cues: No excess residue remains and the loaded drawer opens and closes smoothly.
incompletion_cues: Residue remains, contents are not restored, or the loaded drawer is untested or sticky.

query: How do I apply a hand mask treatment?
task: How to apply a hand mask treatment
goal: Apply and remove a hand mask treatment safely, leaving the product absorbed and the hands comfortable.

steps.text: Clean and dry the hands and prepare the hand mask product.
completion_cues: Hands are clean and dry and the mask package or product is ready.
incompletion_cues: Hands are dirty or wet or the product is not prepared.

steps.text: Apply the mask evenly or fit the treatment gloves correctly.
completion_cues: Both hands have even product coverage or are fully seated in the mask gloves.
incompletion_cues: Coverage is incomplete, gloves are misfitted, or product is spilled excessively.

steps.text: Leave the treatment on for the required time while keeping it in place.
completion_cues: The treatment remains correctly applied for the intended duration.
incompletion_cues: The treatment time is incomplete or the mask has slipped or been removed early.

steps.text: Remove the mask and massage in or clean off the remaining product as directed.
completion_cues: The mask is removed and remaining product is evenly absorbed or removed as directed.
incompletion_cues: The mask remains on or excess product is untreated and the hands are not finished.

query: How do I tighten a loose handrail?
goal: Tighten and stabilize a loose handrail and verify that it safely resists movement.

steps.text: Inspect the handrail, locate loose brackets or fasteners, and prepare the correct tools.
completion_cues: Every loose point is identified and the required tools and access are ready.
incompletion_cues: The loose point is unknown or tools and safe access are not prepared.

steps.text: Support and align the handrail and tighten the existing fasteners.
completion_cues: The rail and brackets are aligned and all serviceable fasteners are snug.
incompletion_cues: The rail is unsupported or misaligned or fasteners remain loose or are overtightened.

steps.text: Repair or reinforce any stripped, missing, or inadequate attachment.
completion_cues: Damaged attachments are replaced or reinforced into sound material.
incompletion_cues: A stripped, missing, or weak attachment remains unresolved.

steps.text: Inspect and load-test the handrail along its full length.
completion_cues: The rail remains rigid under controlled pressure and all hardware is secure.
incompletion_cues: The rail still moves, hardware is unchecked, or no controlled load test is completed.

query: How do I make a friendship bracelet knot pattern?
task: Making a friendship bracelet knot pattern
goal: Make a friendship bracelet with a consistent repeated knot pattern and secure ends.

steps.text: Choose the color order, cut the strands, align them, and secure the starting end.
completion_cues: Correctly ordered equal-working-length strands are anchored with a secure starting loop or knot.
incompletion_cues: Strands are missing, tangled, badly sized, incorrectly ordered, or unsecured.

steps.text: Form the first full repeat of the chosen knot sequence.
completion_cues: One correct, tight, and recognizable pattern repeat is complete.
incompletion_cues: Knots are missing, reversed, loose, or out of sequence.

steps.text: Continue the repeated knot sequence while maintaining tension and width.
completion_cues: The bracelet reaches the required length with a consistent pattern and edges.
incompletion_cues: The pattern is too short, distorted, uneven, or contains unresolved knot errors.

steps.text: Secure the finishing end and test the bracelet fit and knots.
completion_cues: The end is firmly tied or finished and the bracelet fits and remains intact under a gentle test.
incompletion_cues: The end is loose, unfinished, incorrectly sized, or untested.

query: How do I identify mixtures and solutions?
goal: Correctly identify examples as mixtures or solutions and explain the observable basis for the classification.

steps.text: Define mixtures and solutions and identify the samples or examples to compare.
completion_cues: The two categories and all examples under consideration are clearly established.
incompletion_cues: The category meanings or examples are missing or confused.

steps.text: Observe or test whether each example is uniform and whether components remain distinguishable.
completion_cues: Relevant uniformity and component evidence is recorded for every example.
incompletion_cues: Examples are not examined or essential observations are missing.

steps.text: Classify each example using the collected evidence.
completion_cues: Every example is assigned consistently to mixture or solution.
incompletion_cues: One or more examples are unclassified or conflict with the evidence.

steps.text: Check the classifications and explain the reasoning clearly.
completion_cues: The classifications are reviewed and supported by correct, understandable evidence.
incompletion_cues: The reasoning is absent, inconsistent, or scientifically incorrect.

query: How do I make a yogurt parfait?
goal: Assemble a balanced yogurt parfait with distinct layers and ready-to-eat toppings.

steps.text: Prepare a serving container and portion the yogurt as the base layer.
completion_cues: A suitable container holds an even first layer of yogurt.
incompletion_cues: The container or yogurt base is missing, spilled, or poorly portioned.

steps.text: Prepare and add a fruit layer.
completion_cues: Ready-to-eat fruit is evenly distributed over the yogurt.
incompletion_cues: Fruit is unprepared, missing, spoiled, or unevenly added.

steps.text: Add granola and any remaining yogurt, fruit, or chosen toppings in layers.
completion_cues: The intended ingredients form balanced, distinct layers with dry toppings protected from excess moisture.
incompletion_cues: Essential layers are missing, severely uneven, or mixed unintentionally.

steps.text: Finish the top, check the portion, and serve the parfait.
completion_cues: The parfait is complete, neat, and ready to eat with a serving utensil.
incompletion_cues: The serving is unfinished, unstable, or missing the final utensil or presentation check.

query: How do I create a gift bag from wrapping paper?
task: Creating a gift bag from wrapping paper
goal: Fold wrapping paper into a sturdy gift bag with a secure base and finished top.

steps.text: Measure and cut a suitable rectangle of wrapping paper.
completion_cues: A clean paper rectangle is large enough for the intended bag and overlaps correctly.
incompletion_cues: The paper is unmeasured, uncut, damaged, or incorrectly sized.

steps.text: Fold and secure the side seam to form the bag body.
completion_cues: The paper forms a straight tube with a firmly sealed side seam.
incompletion_cues: The body is open, crooked, weakly joined, or glued shut in the wrong area.

steps.text: Fold, open, and seal the bottom gusset and base.
completion_cues: The base lies flat and all bottom flaps overlap and are securely sealed.
incompletion_cues: The bottom is open, misfolded, weak, or unable to stand flat.

steps.text: Finish and reinforce the top and add handles or a closure if desired.
completion_cues: The top edge is neat and reinforced and the completed bag opens, stands, and carries a light test item.
incompletion_cues: The top is raw or weak, handles are insecure, or the bag is untested.

query: How do I decorate a mirror with adhesive gems?
task: Decorating a mirror with adhesive gems
goal: Decorate a mirror with a deliberate, securely adhered pattern of gems while keeping the reflective area clean.

steps.text: Clean and dry the mirror and choose the gem layout and border area.
completion_cues: The attachment surface is clean and dry and a complete layout is planned.
incompletion_cues: The surface is dirty or damp or the design area and pattern are undecided.

steps.text: Lay out or mark the gem pattern before permanent placement.
completion_cues: Gem spacing, sequence, and alignment are established across the intended area.
incompletion_cues: The pattern is incomplete, crowded, uneven, or unplanned.

steps.text: Peel and attach the gems in the planned order, pressing each securely.
completion_cues: All intended gems are attached in alignment with firm contact.
incompletion_cues: Gems remain loose, missing, misaligned, or attached to the wrong area.

steps.text: Inspect adhesion and alignment and clean the finished mirror.
completion_cues: The full design is secure and aligned and the reflective area is clean and usable.
incompletion_cues: Gems lift or shift, the pattern is unfinished, or residue remains on the mirror.

query: How do I calculate the expected value of a probability distribution?
task: Calculate expected value of a probability distribution
goal: Calculate and interpret the expected value of a discrete probability distribution correctly.

steps.text: List every possible outcome and its corresponding probability.
completion_cues: All outcomes and correctly matched probabilities are present in a usable table or list.
incompletion_cues: Outcomes or probabilities are missing, duplicated, or mismatched.

steps.text: Check that the probabilities are valid and sum to one.
completion_cues: Each probability is valid and their total is verified as one, allowing for stated rounding.
incompletion_cues: Probabilities are invalid, unchecked, or do not sum to one without explanation.

steps.text: Multiply each outcome value by its probability.
completion_cues: A correct weighted product is computed for every outcome.
incompletion_cues: One or more products are missing or calculated with the wrong value or probability.

steps.text: Sum the weighted products and interpret the expected value.
completion_cues: The products are summed correctly and the result is explained as a long-run average in context.
incompletion_cues: The final sum is wrong, missing, or interpreted as a guaranteed single outcome.

query: How do I adjust a belt to the right fit?
task: How to adjust a belt to the right fit
goal: Adjust a belt to a secure, comfortable fit and verify that the buckle and strap sit correctly.

steps.text: Put on or measure the belt and identify the needed length and adjustment mechanism.
completion_cues: The current fit and the correct adjustment point or hole are identified.
incompletion_cues: The belt is not measured on the wearer or the adjustment mechanism is misunderstood.

steps.text: Release the buckle or keeper and shorten, lengthen, or rethread the strap.
completion_cues: The strap length is changed correctly without twists or damage.
incompletion_cues: The strap remains unchanged, twisted, incorrectly threaded, or damaged.

steps.text: Refasten the buckle and arrange the belt through its loops or keepers.
completion_cues: The buckle is secure and the strap lies flat through the intended loops and keepers.
incompletion_cues: The buckle is open or misfastened or the strap hangs or twists incorrectly.

steps.text: Check comfort, security, and movement and make a final correction if needed.
completion_cues: The belt stays secure without pinching or excessive looseness during normal movement.
incompletion_cues: The fit is uncomfortable, loose, overly tight, or untested.

query: How do I make tomato basil one-pot pasta?
goal: Cook a tomato basil one-pot pasta with tender pasta and a cohesive, well-seasoned sauce.

steps.text: Gather, wash, measure, and chop the pasta ingredients.
completion_cues: All ingredients are prepared in the needed amounts and the pot and utensils are ready.
incompletion_cues: Ingredients or equipment are missing, unwashed, unmeasured, or uncut.

steps.text: Combine the pasta, tomato components, liquid, and initial seasonings in the pot.
completion_cues: The intended ingredients and enough cooking liquid are evenly combined in one pot.
incompletion_cues: An essential ingredient or liquid is missing or proportions are visibly unsuitable.

steps.text: Bring to a simmer and cook while stirring until the pasta is tender and sauce thickens.
completion_cues: The pasta reaches the intended tenderness and the sauce coats it without sticking or excess liquid.
incompletion_cues: The pasta is hard or mushy, sauce is watery or dry, or the pot is sticking or unmonitored.

steps.text: Add basil and final seasoning, rest briefly, and serve.
completion_cues: Fresh basil and final seasoning are incorporated and the finished pasta is portioned ready to eat.
incompletion_cues: Final flavoring, consistency check, resting, or serving is incomplete.

query: How do I sort animals by habitat?
goal: Sort animals into the correct habitat categories and explain the placement using relevant traits.

steps.text: Define the habitat categories and gather the animal examples to sort.
completion_cues: All habitat groups and animal examples are visible and clearly identified.
incompletion_cues: Categories or animal examples are missing or ambiguous.

steps.text: Identify each animal and note traits or needs relevant to habitat.
completion_cues: Every animal has enough relevant evidence for a habitat decision.
incompletion_cues: Animals are unidentified or important habitat traits are not considered.

steps.text: Place each animal into the most appropriate habitat group.
completion_cues: Every animal is assigned to a habitat consistently with its traits and needs.
incompletion_cues: Animals remain unsorted or are placed in incompatible habitats.

steps.text: Review the groups, correct mistakes, and explain the classifications.
completion_cues: All groups are checked and each placement has a clear, correct explanation.
incompletion_cues: Errors remain or explanations are absent or inconsistent with the habitat.

query: How do I install adhesive utility hooks?
goal: Install adhesive utility hooks in suitable positions and verify that they remain secure under an appropriate load.

steps.text: Choose and mark suitable hook positions and clean and dry the mounting surfaces.
completion_cues: Each location is suitable, aligned, marked, and clean and dry.
incompletion_cues: Locations are unsuitable or unmarked or the surfaces remain dirty or damp.

steps.text: Prepare and apply each adhesive strip to the correct hook or surface orientation.
completion_cues: The adhesive is correctly oriented, fully contacting the hook and mounting surface, and free of contamination.
incompletion_cues: The liner, orientation, contact, or adhesive handling is incorrect.

steps.text: Press and mount the hooks firmly and allow the specified bond time.
completion_cues: The hooks are level, firmly pressed for the required time, and left unloaded for curing.
incompletion_cues: Hooks are crooked, insufficiently pressed, disturbed, or loaded before the bond is ready.

steps.text: Inspect and gently load-test the cured hooks with suitable items.
completion_cues: The hooks remain aligned and attached under an appropriate test load.
incompletion_cues: A hook lifts, shifts, is overloaded, or has not been tested after curing.
